response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack engineer with broad polyglot experience, but the role requires 8 years of C++ programming and 5 years building large-scale infrastructure/distributed systems with compute/storage/hardware focus. The resume shows only a brief ~6-month C/C++ stint at National Instruments (2014-2015) and a bachelor thesis using C++. Primary expertise is in TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web/product engineering, not cloud compute platforms or C++ systems work. No demonstrated technical leadership of a cloud compute platform or distributed-systems architecture at scale.
strengths: Long tenure as a senior/staff engineer with mentoring, workshops, and community teaching experience
Some exposure to high-throughput, low-latency micro-services (Dunnhumby) and Kubernetes-deployed platforms
Demonstrated ownership of open-source projects, frameworks, and developer tooling
concerns: Far short of 8 years of C++ experience — only a brief NI role and an academic project
No evidence of large-scale cloud compute platform or distributed-systems architecture leadership
Core stack is web/product (TS/Node/DLang), misaligned with C++ infrastructure focus

key_factors.factor: Lacks cloud compute platform / distributed-systems leadership
Strong mentoring and cross-team technical leadership track record
